name: THE DARK KNIGHT — PEAK HUMAN PROTOCOL
combat_style: Multi-discipline: boxing + Muay Thai + clinch/grappling + defensive counter — the all-rounder
flagship_note: The app's hardest, most comprehensive campaign — the only one integrating all SIX pillars: gymnastics, calisthenics, strength, cardio/conditioning, fight (conditioning + technical), and parkour. Because no real program safely fuses six pillars at high volume, this campaign actively MANAGES concurrent-training load (pillar rotation, deloads, enhanced gates) — see campaign.safety_scaffolding.
fantasy: Forge a peak-human all-rounder — the body of an acrobat, the strength of a fighter, the movement of a traceur, and the discipline to master all of it without breaking.
core_rule: The body is a tool — sharpen it, don't shatter it.
core_rule_enforcement: Six pillars at high volume is the real overtraining risk. This campaign never stacks heavy strength + high-impact parkour + gymnastics in one session, prescribes deloads, gates advanced/high-risk skills behind progressions, and enforces parkour/gymnastics safety. Discipline means recovering as hard as you train — reckless volume is failure, not virtue.
sourcing_note: Grounded in the deep-research report (protocol-src/research/darkknight-research-report.md). VERIFIED: fan calisthenics structure (SuperheroJacked 5-day A/B + core + cardio; Darebee bodyweight circuit), the parkour beginner progression (landing / roll-from-squat / precision jumps with 1-2 ft targets and cleared landing / safety vaults; flips deferred to advanced), and peer-reviewed gymnastics wrist-injury data (~53% adolescent prevalence, up to 75% chronic in male gymnasts) mandating wrist prehab + pinch-pain screening. IMPORTANT: research confirmed NO existing program fuses six pillars and NO source covers safe six-pillar periodization — that fusion is this campaign's design, built conservatively. One research sub-source (a karate blog) was flagged for suspicious activity and EXCLUDED; martial-arts content uses the app's existing disciplines instead. The '~127 martial arts' Batman claim was not substantiated and is treated as comic lore, not a training target.

parkour: Balance and wall work — NO flips prescribed

fight_focus: Multi-discipline flow (strike into clinch into control)

fight.requirements: basic strikes, beginner movement drills, lowest/closest parkour targets, wrist prehab
combinations, intermediate movement, precision jumps + safety vaults, handstand/ring progressions
advanced combinations, full movement integration; advanced gymnastics skills only after progression gates; experienced athletes

hard_never_requires: Flips or advanced acrobatics (backflips, wall flips) — deferred, never auto-prescribed
Parkour on hard/high surfaces or big gaps
Training through wrist pinch-pain or joint pain
Stacking all pillars heavy in one day

overtraining_management.why: No source covers safe six-pillar periodization — the research's flagged core risk. This campaign manages it explicitly.
rules: PILLAR ROTATION: never stack heavy strength + high-impact parkour + gymnastics load in the same session; each session leads with one primary pillar and keeps the others light.
DELOAD: after each hard-camp block, a lighter recovery stage/week is prescribed; skipping it is discouraged (and completing it earns an achievement).
ENHANCED READINESS GATE on integration/peak stages (10-12): normal readiness plus joint-soreness and sleep-debt questions; poor answers push toward Easy or a movement-quality day.
This is the hardest campaign — predetermined targets, no max-until-collapse, and rest floors on peak stages.
START LOW: beginner precision-jump targets ~1-2 ft away; progress distance/height slowly.
CLEARED LANDING: confirm the landing area is clear and safe before any jump.
LANDING MECHANICS: bend knees to absorb impact, land balls-of-feet to heel, quiet landings.
ROLL FROM A SQUAT: feet shoulder-width, head tucked to chest, roll diagonally over one shoulder to disperse impact.
NO FLIPS / ADVANCED ACROBATICS prescribed — framed as advanced 'beyond the basics', deferred, never auto-assigned.
Forgiving surfaces (grass/mats) for beginners; never train new drills fatigued.

gymnastics_safety.why: Wrist pain is highly prevalent in gymnastics (~53% adolescent prevalence; up to 75% chronic in male gymnasts) — peer-reviewed.
rules: DAILY WRIST PREHAB: soft-tissue work (lacrosse ball) + wrist mobility before ring/handstand/bar work.
SCREEN FOR PINCH-PAIN: front-of-wrist 'pinch' pain is an early warning sign — stop that work and regress if it appears.
CONNECTIVE-TISSUE PREP: build support holds and straight-arm strength before loading levers/skills.
SKILL GATES: levers, muscle-ups and advanced ring skills unlock only after their progression is cleared — never assigned cold.

safety_scaffolding.fantasy_flags: Batman's '127 martial arts' and peak-human comic feats are lore only, shown with a '⚠ comic fantasy — not a training goal' tag. The real lesson is disciplined, well-rounded, safe training.

equipment_profiles.rule: Every stage runs on at least the MINIMAL version. Rings substitute to bar/TRX/floor; parkour needs only floor targets + a low step; martial arts run on a bag or shadow.

voice_guidance.policy: The timer SPEAKS the workout so the user never has to read the screen. Every movement is announced by name as it begins, with a short form/coach cue, a spoken countdown into work and rest, round start/end calls, rep or time counts, a halfway call, a final-10-seconds call, and the module key safety cue. Striking combinations are called out loud in rhythm.
voice_guidance.source_fields: Spoken lines are generated from each module title, exercises[]/drills[], coach_note, rounds[].goal/length, and the campaign safety cues — no on-screen reading required.
voice_guidance.note: Shared voice-guidance contract (see specs/22-voice-guidance.md) — embedded in every campaign so the timer is fully audio-guided. Where a campaign defines its own cadence/count (e.g. Gravity voice_count_mode), voice guidance defers to it for counting and layers the rest.

achievements.name: Aerial Control
achievements.trigger: Clear the gymnastics stages with no wrist-pain flags

achievements.name: Traceur
achievements.trigger: Clear the parkour stages with clean landings and rolls

achievements.name: Discipline
achievements.trigger: Complete every prescribed deload / recovery day

achievements.name: Become the Bat
achievements.trigger: Defeat Stage 12 without a control-breakdown fail

achievements.name: Peak Human
achievements.trigger: Clear Fit and Fight for all 12 stages

response: Add wrist prehab and reduce gymnastics load
Drop Fight difficulty by one level
Do NOT automatically lower the Fit path

final_boss.fight_trial_name: Become the Bat — The Peak Human Trial
final_boss.control_mechanic: The all-pillar trial: twelve rounds cycling every pillar (gymnastic control, calisthenic power, strength, conditioning, each fight discipline, movement/parkour). Form and control are gated — sloppy or unsafe execution fails the round. Mastery = all six pillars, in control, composed.
round_objectives: Muay Thai round — kicks, knees, clinch
Gymnastic control — handstand/ring hold or balance
Movement round — landing, roll, precision (safe, low)
Become the Bat — finish in complete control

categories: Handstand or ring-support hold for time
Weighted or advanced calisthenic strength target
fit_final_test.rule: Predetermined targets only — never max-until-collapse. Parkour test stays low and cleared; gymnastics test respects wrist prehab and pinch-pain stop.

art.art_note: Placeholder banner reuse — swap for an original Dark-Knight-inspired asset before launch (IP).
